perturbation.original_text: Definitions 1.1 In this Agreement, including the recitals, except as expressly provided or unless the context otherwise requires, (a) Affiliate means, in respect of a party hereto, a corporation which is the subsidiary of the party or vice versa or where each of the party and the corporation is controlled by the same person, (b) Area of Non-Competition means the State of Zacatecas, Mexico and the area extending 20 kilometers in all directions from the external boundary of each mineral property owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest, in Mexico, as at the Effective Date, (c) Business Day means a day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or a civic or statutory holiday in Reno, Nevada and Vancouver, British Columbia, (d) Closing means the completion of the transactions contemplated by the Arrangement Agreement, (e) Designated Mineral Property means a mineral property or individual mineral concession within a mineral property, that is owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest in Mexico as of the Effective Date, (f) Effective Date means May 3, 200ri or such later date as determined under the Arrangement Agreement, (g) Mining Activities means any acquisition of mineral rights or any mineral exploration or development activities, in any manner whatsoever, and (h) Person means an individual, corporation, body corporate, firm, limited liability company, parmership, syndicate, joint venture, society, association, trust or unincorporated organization.
perturbation.changed_text: Definitions 1.1 In this Agreement, including the recitals, except as expressly provided or unless the context otherwise requires, (a) Affiliate means, in respect of a party hereto, a corporation which is the subsidiary of the party or vice versa or where each of the party and the corporation is controlled by the same person, (b) Area of Non-Competition means the State of Zacatecas, Mexico, (c) Business Day means a day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or a civic or statutory holiday in Reno, Nevada and Vancouver, British Columbia, (d) Closing means the completion of the transactions contemplated by the Arrangement Agreement, (e) Designated Mineral Property means a mineral property or individual mineral concession within a mineral property, that is owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest in Mexico as of the Effective Date, (f) Effective Date means May 3, 200ri or such later date as determined under the Arrangement Agreement, (g) Mining Activities means any acquisition of mineral rights or any mineral exploration or development activities, in any manner whatsoever, and (h) Person means an individual, corporation, body corporate, firm, limited liability company, parmership, syndicate, joint venture, society, association, trust or unincorporated organization.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Area of Non-Competition' is changed to ONLY include the State of Zacatecas, Mexico, omitting 'and the area extending 20 kilometers in all directions from the external boundary of each mineral property owned or controlled by Western Silver or in which Western Silver holds any legal interest, in Mexico, as at the Effective Date'. This contradicts the subsequent mention of the original definition of 'Area of Non-Competition' in Section 2.1(b), causing confusion and uncertainty regarding the actual geographical scope of the non-competition clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Western Copper covenants and agrees with Glamis that, for a period of 2 years after the Effective Date, it will not, directly or indirectly, either individually or in partnership or jointly or in conjunction with any Person, which will include being a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor of such Person or in any other manner whatsoever, (a) carry on or be engaged in Mining Activities, or i 162967.3 -4- (b) advise, lend money to, guarantee the debts or obligations of or permit its name to be used by any Person who carries on or is engaged in Mining Activities, in the Area of Non-Competition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Area of Non-Competition' by removing the '20 kilometers' clause. Section 2.1(b) references the original broader definition of 'Area of Non-Competition'. Therefore, the modified definition in the changed text directly contradicts Section 2.1(b), creating a conflict in the scope of the non-competition clause. This is a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces ambiguity and affects the enforceability of the non-competition agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Right of First Offer 2.2 Glamis covenants and agrees with Western Copper that if at any time it intends to dispose of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment, it will give Western Copper notice (the ' Disposition Notice") of the intended disposition. For a period of 30 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the ease of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property within the 30 day period, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees til in its absolute discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a period of 3 months from the first to occur of the date that Glamis and Western Copper acknowledge failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 30 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis wall, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.changed_text: Right of First Offer 2.2 Glamis covenants and agrees with Western Copper that if at any time it intends to dispose of a Designated Mineral Property for cash consideration or by abandonment, it will give Western Copper notice (the 'Disposition Notice') of the intended disposition. For a period of 30 days from the time of delivery of the Disposition Notice Glamis will, if requested by Western Silver, entertain an offer from Western Copper to acquire the Designated Mineral Property. In the ease of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to dispose of for cash consideration, the parties will negotiate to reach a potentially agreeable agreement for the sale to Western Copper of the [Designated Mineral Property. If Glamis and Western Copper are unable to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property, Glamis may thereafter dispose of the Designated Mineral Property as it sees fit in its sole and unfettered discretion. If Glamis does not dispose of the Designated Mineral Property within a commercially reasonable period from the date of failure to negotiate an acceptable agreement with respect to the Designated Mineral Property and the end of the 30 day period, the provisions of this section 2.2 will once again apply to any intended disposition of the Designated Mineral Property by Glamis. In the case of a Designated Mineral Property that Glamis intends to abandon, Glamis will, if requested by Western Copper, transfer such Designated Mineral Property to Western Copper at no cost save and except for the reasonable costs of transfer incurred by Glamis.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'negotiate in good faith to reach a mutually agreeable agreement' is replaced with 'negotiate to reach a potentially agreeable agreement.' Further, 'absolute discretion' is changed to 'sole and unfettered discretion,' and 'period of 3 months' is changed to 'commercially reasonable period'. This perturbation introduces ambiguity around the standard of negotiation required, potentially making it less enforceable and subjective, as well as making it uncertain what time period does Glamis have to dispose the property to re-engage section 2.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: Glamis acknowledges and agrees that a breach of its covenants contained in this Agreement would result in damage to Western Copper that could not adequately be compensated 1162967.3 -5- ibr by monetary award alone. Accordingly, Glamis agrees that in the event of any such breach, in addition to any other remedies available to Western Copper at law or otherwise, Western Copper will be entitled, as a matter of right and without the need to prove damage, to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for relief by way of injunction, restraining order, decree or otherwise as may be appropriate to ensure compliance by Glamis with the provisions of this Agreement and to restrain any breach of this Agreement by Glamis. Any remedy expressly set forth in this §3.2 will be in addition to and not inclusive of or dependent upon the exercise of any other remedy available to Western Copper at law or otherwise
perturbation.justification: The changes introduce ambiguity regarding the standard of negotiation required ('potentially agreeable agreement' vs 'mutually agreeable agreement') and the timeframe for disposition ('commercially reasonable period' vs 'period of 3 months'). This weakens the 'covenants contained in this Agreement' mentioned in the contradicted text, making it harder to prove a breach and obtain remedies like injunctions. The original text provided more concrete and enforceable terms, while the modified text makes the obligations more subjective and uncertain, directly contradicting the clause that assumes a clear and enforceable agreement. Further, the change from 'absolute discretion' to 'sole and unfettered discretion' adds additional ambiguity. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Glamis' Right of TerminaHon 3.6 Glamis may, at its option, terminate this Agreement by written notice to Western Copper, effective immediately upon delivery of the notice, should Western Copper cease conducting business in the normal course, become insolvent, make a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, suffer or permit the appointment of a receiver for its business or assets or avail itself of, or become subject to, any proceedings under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act (Canada) or any other statute of any province or state relating to insolvency or the protection of rights of creditors.
perturbation.changed_text: Glamis' Right of Termination 3.6 Glamis may, at its sole discretion, terminate this Agreement by written notice to Western Copper, effective upon delivery of the notice, should Western Copper experience financial hardship.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the specific conditions under which Glamis can terminate the agreement with the vague term 'financial hardship' creates ambiguity. The original agreement lists objective and verifiable events (insolvency, receivership, etc.), making termination conditions clear. The new language offers no concrete definition of 'financial hardship', making it difficult to determine when Glamis can rightfully terminate the agreement, and creating uncertainty for Western Copper. It also contradicts the essence of Section 3.3, which states that all restrictions in the Agreement are reasonable and valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of Western Copper and Glamis agree that all restrictions in this Agreement applicable to them are reasonable and valid, and all defences to the strict enforcement thereof by Western Copper or Glamis, as the case may be, arc hereby waived by them.
perturbation.justification: The change from specific, verifiable conditions for termination (insolvency, receivership) to the vague term 'financial hardship' directly contradicts the statement in the contradicted text that 'all restrictions in this Agreement applicable to them are reasonable and valid'. 'Financial hardship' lacks a clear definition, making it subjective and potentially unreasonable, thereby creating a contradiction regarding the enforceability and validity of the termination clause.
